title: Coherent Pixel Typeface: Distinct Yet On-Style
strategies: trivial baseline: a low-distance arm code (consecutive glyphs differ in a single row) -- coherent but easily confused
distance-only greedy: random 4-connected glyphs, greedily kept to maximize min pairwise Hamming distance -- distinct but noise-like, huge motif vocabulary

ECC reformulation (strong): fix one stroke motif (full-row arms on a shared spine), then place glyphs as an even-weight code so every pair differs in >=2 rows -- distinctness and a tiny vocabulary at once
open headroom: richer motif bases or nonlinear codes can push distance higher while keeping vocabulary small, above the reference strong solution
